history.company: New York State Office of Information Technology Services
history.title: Director, Design Systems and Accessibility
history.summary: Proposed the NYS Design System to executive leadership. Hired a cross-discipline team of 14. Built the component library, the token architecture, and the accessibility compliance program.
history.description: The design system was included in the Governor's 2024 State of the State address. I built the initial component library — 25+ web components in Lit and TypeScript, a token architecture on the DTCG standard, and a custom elements manifest that serves as the single source of truth for design, code, docs, and AI tooling. Built and published an MCP server that exposes component specs, tokens, and validation rules to LLMs — turns out structured context makes AI-generated code consistent and accessible without relying on probabilistic generation. On the accessibility side, I led the formation of NYS's Central Accessibility Team ahead of federal (2026) and state (2027) WCAG deadlines. Co-authored updates to the state's accessibility policy and contributed to revisions of State Technology Law. I still review PRs and write code daily.

history.title: Founder & Principal Consultant
history.summary: Design and engineering consultancy — bridging the gap between design and development for organizations that care about getting it right.
history.description: Hello Patient: design system strategy for a health startup — aligned Figma variables with Tailwind architecture, ran workshops with designers, developers, and leadership to establish component APIs and a governance process. Asurion: embedded with content and product teams for six months during a company-wide CMS and design system build. Harvard Business School's Digital Initiative: helped the team articulate its mission, then designed and built an edition-based content platform and an interactive learning experience. Additional clients include Boeing, Delta, Fox, the Committee to Protect Journalists, and others.

history.title: VP, Technology
history.summary: Technical docs and training for Fox's composable content platform. Directed front-end teams building for the Super Bowl and World Cup.
history.description: Built documentation and onboarding systems for Spark, Fox's proprietary composable content platform on AWS. Directed front-end engineering teams building applications for global live events. Managed the launch of Fox Weather. Ran week-long planning sessions with 80+ people and standardized processes between back-end and front-end teams across Sports, News, Weather, and Entertainment.

history.title: Associate Director, Business Development
history.summary: Talking to prospective clients, figuring out what they actually needed, and scoping the work to get them there.
history.description: Eight months on the business development side — meeting with prospective clients, listening to their problems, and putting together proposals that made sense for both sides. It changed how I think about scoping work. You learn a lot about what matters to people when your job is to listen before you pitch.

history.title: VP, Technology
history.summary: Sole engineer. Built and maintained the entire stack — branding, UX, CMS, mobile app, infrastructure. Grew the site from 1M to 20M monthly pageviews before acquisition.
history.description: I handled everything that touched a screen: branding, UX, custom development, mobile app, server infrastructure, caching, CDN. Conducted user interviews and used those insights to update designs and run experiments. Built an iOS app that Apple featured in the App Store. Migrated the platform from Movable Type to WordPress. Simply Recipes was acquired by Fexy Media in 2016.

history.title: Director, Platform Development
history.summary: Consolidated four divergent CMS platforms — AMC, WEtv, IFC, Sundance — into a unified composable site architecture.
history.description: Every channel was running its own CMS with its own tech strategy. I met with stakeholders across each brand, defined the requirements for a shared framework, and built it. Created and deployed a comprehensive analytics strategy across all business units.

history.title: Board Member
history.summary: Served on the board of Melody, a community-governed fork of Movable Type focused on open-source publishing.
history.description: Melody was a fork of Movable Type Open Source — same foundation, but governed by the community instead of a corporation. The idea was to build something nimble: take risks faster, incorporate a wider range of contributions, and let the people who actually use the software steer where it goes. I joined the board because I trusted the people involved and believed in making publishing tools that anyone could freely benefit from.
